test.title: JEE Main 2026 (2 April) — Shift 1
test.description: Official NTA B.Tech paper, 2 April 2026 Shift 1. This file is a TEXT-ONLY SUBSET (figure/diagram and match-the-list questions omitted). The source PDF has no answer key, so correctAnswer values are computed and must be verified against the official NTA key.

questions.subject: MATHEMATICS
questions.questionText: Let $\alpha$ and $\alpha+2$ (with $\alpha \in \mathbb{Z}$) be the roots of the quadratic equation $x(x+2) + (x+1)(x+3) + (x+2)(x+4) + \cdots + (x+n-1)(x+n+1) = 4n$ for some $n \in \mathbb{N}$. Then $n + \alpha$ is equal to:
questions.explanation: Each term (x+k-1)(x+k+1) = (x+k)^2 - 1, so the sum is Σ(x+k)^2 - n = 4n, i.e. Σ_{k=1}^{n}(x+k)^2 = 5n. This is a quadratic in x whose roots differ by 2; requiring (sum)^2 - 4(product) = 4 gives n = 7, and then α = -5, so n + α = 2.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: MATHEMATICS
questions.questionText: Let A be the set of the first 101 terms of an A.P. with first term 1 and common difference 5, and let B be the set of the first 71 terms of an A.P. with first term 9 and common difference 7. The number of elements of A ∩ B that are divisible by 3 is:
questions.explanation: A reaches 501 and B reaches 499. Common terms form an A.P. starting at 16 with common difference 35 (LCM of 5 and 7): 16, 51, 86, ..., up to 499 (terms 16 + 35m, m = 0..13). Those divisible by 3 require 1 + 2m ≡ 0 (mod 3), i.e. m ≡ 1 (mod 3): m = 1, 4, 7, 10, 13 — that is 5 terms.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: MATHEMATICS
questions.questionText: The number of seven-digit numbers that can be formed using the digits 1, 2, 3, 5 and 7 such that each digit is used at least once is:
questions.explanation: This is the number of onto functions from 7 places to 5 digits: 5^7 - C(5,1)4^7 + C(5,2)3^7 - C(5,3)2^7 + C(5,4)1^7 = 78125 - 81920 + 21870 - 1280 + 5 = 16800.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: The dimensional formula of $\tfrac{1}{2}\varepsilon_0 E^{2}$ (where $\varepsilon_0$ is the permittivity of vacuum and E is the electric field) is $M^{a} L^{b} T^{c}$. The value of $2a - b + c$ is:
questions.explanation: (1/2)ε_0 E^2 is energy density = energy/volume, with dimensions M L^{-1} T^{-2}. So a = 1, b = -1, c = -2, and 2a - b + c = 2 - (-1) + (-2) = 1.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: A planet P1 orbits a star of mass 2M in an orbit of radius R, while another planet P2 orbits a different star of mass 4M in an orbit of radius 2R. The ratio of the time periods of revolution of P2 and P1 is:
questions.explanation: From Kepler's third law T^2 ∝ R^3 / M. So (T2/T1)^2 = ((2R)^3/4M) / (R^3/2M) = (8/4)(2) = 4, giving T2/T1 = 2.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: The equation of a plane progressive wave is $y = 5\cos\pi\left(200t - \dfrac{x}{150}\right)$, where x and y are in cm and t is in seconds. The velocity of the wave is ____ m/s.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: The angular momentum of an electron in a hydrogen atom is $\dfrac{3h}{\pi}$. The energy of the electron is ____ eV.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: A liquid drop of diameter 2 mm breaks into 512 droplets. The change in surface energy is $\alpha \times 10^{-6}$ J. The value of $\alpha$ is (take surface tension of the liquid = 0.08 N/m):
questions.explanation: Drop radius R = 1 mm; with 512 droplets each radius r = R/512^(1/3) = R/8. ΔE = T·4π(512 r^2 - R^2) = T·4π(8R^2 - R^2) = 28π T R^2 = 28π(0.08)(10^-3)^2 ≈ 7.0 x 10^-6 J, so α ≈ 7.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: PHYSICS
questions.questionText: In a single-slit diffraction pattern, the wavelength of light used is 628 nm and the slit width is 0.2 mm. The angular width of the central maximum is $\alpha \times 10^{-2}$ degrees. The value of $\alpha$ is:
questions.explanation: Angular width of the central maximum = 2λ/a = 2(628 x 10^-9)/(0.2 x 10^-3) = 6.28 x 10^-3 rad = 0.36 degrees = 36 x 10^-2 degrees.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: CHEMISTRY
questions.questionText: The mass of iron converted into Fe3O4 by the action of 18 g of steam (iron present in excess) is: (Molar masses of H, O, Fe are 1, 16 and 56 g/mol)
questions.explanation: 3Fe + 4H2O -> Fe3O4 + 4H2. 18 g of water = 1 mol, which reacts with (3/4) mol Fe = 0.75 x 56 = 42 g of iron.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: CHEMISTRY
questions.questionText: What is the energy (in J/atom) required for the process Li^2+ (g) -> Li^3+ (g) + e^-? (Take the ionization energy of the H atom in the ground state as 2.18 x 10^-18 J/atom.)
questions.explanation: Li^2+ is hydrogen-like with Z = 3. Ionization energy scales as Z^2: E = 2.18 x 10^-18 x 9 = 1.962 x 10^-17 J/atom.
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA

questions.subject: CHEMISTRY
questions.questionText: 19.5 g of fluoroacetic acid (molar mass 78 g/mol) is dissolved in 500 g of water at 298 K. The depression in the freezing point of water is 1 degree C. What is the Ka of fluoroacetic acid? (Kf of water = 1.86 K kg/mol; assume molarity equals molality.)
questions.sourceName: Official JEE Main 2026 (2 Apr) Shift 1, NTA
